jobDescription: We are notonthehighstreet. Home to over 5000 of the UK’s best small creative businesses, we have been changing the online retail landscape since 2006. Whether you’re working with our Partners to help grow their business, or behind the scenes developing code, we are continuously innovating & working on some really cool stuff all at the same time. And the best bit? We’re making a difference to our Partners’ lives as we do it. What we need We’re on the lookout for a superstar with a real passion for notonthehighstreet to take on the unique role of Pop Up Manager. You will come with a host of skills from managing retail teams to influencing product selection - you will go above and beyond in demonstrating a passion for our brand, unique products and small creative businesses and in their ability to inspire both staff and customers. As the retail environment evolves, online retailers are moving offline to showcase their brand, increase customer awareness and provide customers with a more tangible experience. By creating a destination for customers to experience our product first-hand and hear the stories behind them, we have a strong opportunity to re-establish our unique product proposition and improve consideration to purchase - in store and beyond. Some of the things you will be doing You will be ultimately responsible for the seamless running of the NOTHS Pop Up stores, ensuring sales targets are delivered. In order to excel in this role you will also be involved in much of the logistical planning of the site and will be fully responsible for planning the day to day running of the stores thinking of solutions to overcome any foreseeable issues ensuring there are clear plans in place prior to the stores opening. You will be a true NOTHS ambassador ensuring you have a full understanding of the brand, our values and our USP. You will lead a team who will be able to demonstrate their brand knowledge and love of NOTHS at any time.  The team you lead will excel at customer service ensuring that “ Thoughtful” remains at the heart of every interaction they have in store.  Your understanding of the USP of each product will be second to none, you will be passionate about ensuring these products end up in the hands and homes of customers who understand the USP and value that these have been made by a small creative business. You will think of clever and creative ways to ensure sales targets are met, keeping the store looking on brand whilst ensuring sales and sell through targets are hit.  Responsibilities Include: - Manage the on-site retail team Training and day to day management of a small team to create and deliver the NOTHS customer experience - Achieve commercial and data capture targets Responsible for team meeting daily sales targets Responsible for team capturing as much customer data as possible at all the data touch points (eg.e-receipts/ on site competition) - Manage stock: pre launch Ensuring the stock selected is appropriate for the environment and developing a merchandising plan for this Supporting development of an inventory management programme to track daily sales, returns and replenishment requirements Processing of stock from delivery at warehouse to onsite at each location Sourcing packaging (branded canvas tote bags / tissue paper…) alongside NOTHS team Sharing any recommendations on a potential best fit EPOS system based on NOTHS requirements to help NOTHS source this - Manage stock: on-site Management of requesting, picking and delivering replen stock to each site Visual merchandising of the stock at the pop up Optimising the space on a daily basis to ensure sales targets are met Stock insurance: this will be outsourced, but you will manage Management of transfer of EPOS data to NOTHS internal system at the end of both pop ups - Communicate daily with NOTHS internal team Tracking and providing commentary on daily sales, returns & sell thru rates and engagement data Feedback on any issues, suggesting solutions - Manage additional location requirements / ad hoc issues that arise Deal with complaints from customers to maintain the store’s reputation Keep abreast of market trends to determine the need for improvements in the store Manage waste disposal Ensure the store fulfills all legal health and safety guidelines Inspect the areas in the store and resolve any issues that might arise Manage any trading restrictions / changes in trading restrictions etc Our perks It’s important to us that our people are well looked after, which is why we offer everyone BUPA healthcare, life insurance and a pension plan. Because having a break is important for your physical and mental wellbeing, our holiday allowance is 25 days (plus bank holidays) AND your entitlement will increase an extra day for every 2 years you spend with us. Add in our in-office massages, yoga sessions or the abundance of free fruit, you can see that we want our people to feel good inside and out.
